meta.title: Renewal Rush
meta.tone: Fast, witty, enterprise-playful — not cringe, not heavy
meta.oneLiner: At-risk accounts keep surfacing. Deploy Quivly before churn gets there first.
meta.scope: Small — browser Signal Triage board, embeddable on quivly.ai
meta.platformNote: Ship marketing game as embeddable browser arcade (Game Lab → quivly.ai). Keep Unreal as learning sandbox only.

landingPage.headline: Your new post-sales team, without the headcount.
landingPage.subheadline: AI agents that surface and act on churn risks and expansion signals to grow your accounts on autopilot.
landingPage.tagline: The AI workforce for post-sales teams

enterpriseGames.name: PagerTron (incident.io)
enterpriseGames.why: Tiny vibe-coded game on a marketing page — proves craft, fun, shareable

enterpriseGames.name: Product-themed arcade (SaaS gamification)
enterpriseGames.why: Teach the product metaphor through play, not a demo deck

whatWeAreNot: Full game / multiple levels / story campaign

premiseResearch.conclusion: Best B2B marketing games are browser-based, one mechanic, 30–90 seconds, leaderboard — not 3D engines.

benchmarks.name: PagerTron
benchmarks.sourceQuote: Escape the pager duty — Your pager has a paging problem. The tool that wakes you up... breaks. Press Spacebar to Start. VIEW LEADERBOARD.
benchmarks.mechanic: Retro arcade — Press Spacebar to Start; single-button play with high-score leaderboard
benchmarks.story: Escape the pager duty. Your pager has a paging problem — the tool that wakes you up breaks.
benchmarks.whyItWorks: incident.io blog: vibe-coded in Claude Code; simple game with database of high scores on Anthropic landing page

benchmarks.name: Wizlympics
benchmarks.sourceQuote: Use the Wiz Sensor to avoid fake cloud services, like Amazon Prime Rib, and collect real cloud services, like AWS S3. Collecting real services earns 10 points; a fake service results in game over. Hit 500 points to win swag.
benchmarks.mechanic: Collect real cloud services, avoid fakes — one fake = game over; 10 points per correct collect
benchmarks.story: Cloud Security Olympic Games — distinguish real vs fake cloud services with the Wiz Sensor.
benchmarks.whyItWorks: Product metaphor in one sentence (real vs fake cloud); 500 pts unlocks swag form; infinite retry

benchmarks.name: Unicorn Startup Simulator
benchmarks.sourceQuote: Create a Billion Dollar Start-Up or Die Trying. Your goal is to have a valuation of at least $1 billion by the end of the year. Use your time wisely! Also, make sure to keep your employees happy :)
benchmarks.mechanic: Weekly binary decisions (Okay / Nope) — track valuation and employee happiness
benchmarks.story: Create a Billion Dollar Start-Up or Die Trying — reach $1B valuation in one year while keeping employees happy.
benchmarks.whyItWorks: Score = valuation ($); time-management metaphor from Toggl marketing; shareable high score

benchmarks.name: Renewal Rush (Unreal FPS)
benchmarks.sourceQuote: arena-discovery.json: 139 level actors on Lvl_ArenaShooter; studio-state phase loop-polish-pass-2-complete
benchmarks.mechanic: First-person arena, walk + Agent Pulse beam, spawn waves (prior direction)
benchmarks.story: Customer Command arena — tag at-risk account orbs before timer hits zero (prior direction).
benchmarks.whyItStruggles: 139 actors per arena-discovery.json; blueprints + MCP polish — high build cost vs browser embed for 90s demo

pattern: End card with CTA + share screenshot
Embeddable on marketing site — no download

options.title: Signal Triage
options.subtitle: Whack-a-Risk
options.mechanic: At-risk account cards pop up — click/tap to Deploy Agent. Healthy = ignore. Expansion = bonus. Miss at-risk = churn tick.
options.story: Renewal week. Signals flood in faster than your team can triage. One tap — Quivly already drafted the save.
options.oneLiner: At-risk accounts keep surfacing. Deploy Quivly before churn gets there first.

productMapping.connect: Wave intro — signals flood the board
productMapping.see: Each pop shows account name, ARR, health score, usage delta

options.whyWinner: PagerTron-level simplicity; visceral post-sales metaphor; teaches Act step instantly; booth-friendly on any laptop

options.title: Renewal Run
options.subtitle: Endless runner
options.mechanic: Account health bar shrinks as you run renewal runway. Tap to Act (boost health). Dodge noise signals. Collect expansion orbs.
options.story: Renewal runway is shrinking. Every action buys ARR. Miss one, churn wins.
options.oneLiner: Run the renewal runway — act fast or lose the quarter.

productMapping.act: Tap = deploy agent pulse, bar refills

options.whyNotFirst: Fun but less unique — runner genre is crowded; harder to show account-specific detail

options.title: Next Best Action
options.subtitle: Priority picker
options.mechanic: 3 account cards appear. Pick the highest-priority at-risk account before timer expires. Streak for combo multiplier.
options.story: Quivly ranked your queue. Can you triage as fast as the AI?
options.oneLiner: Beat the clock — pick the account that needs you most.

productMapping.score: Health + risk level on each card

options.whyNotFirst: Most faithful to Quivly Actions Feed but less arcade energy — better as round 2 variant

options.title: Signal Sort
options.subtitle: Connect → See → Score → Act lanes
options.mechanic: Falling signal cards — sort into four lanes matching product flow. Wrong lane = penalty.
options.story: Your stack is noisy. Sort every signal before renewal day.
options.oneLiner: Every signal. One place. Sort fast.
options.whyNotFirst: Teaches full product flow but four lanes may confuse booth players in first 5 seconds

justification.summary: Signal Triage beats alternatives on simplicity, metaphor clarity, and booth readiness — same bar as PagerTron and Wizlympics.
justification.vsRenewalRun: Runner genre is crowded; hard to show per-account ARR, health score, and usage delta on a scrolling HUD.
justification.vsNextBestAction: Faithful to Actions Feed but reads as a quiz, not an arcade — better as phase-2 variant after core loop ships.
justification.vsSignalSort: Teaches full Connect→See→Score→Act but four lanes overload first-time players; fails the 5-second metaphor test.
justification.vsUnrealFps: 139-actor arena + blueprints + MCP polish is high build cost for a 90s marketing embed; browser ships faster and embeds on quivly.ai.
justification.productFit: Whack-a-Risk maps directly to post-sales triage: signals surface, you act on at-risk accounts, Quivly drafts the save — mirrors landing copy 'Act, with the draft already written'.

productMapping.connect: Wave intro — CRM, usage, support signals flood the board
productMapping.see: Each card shows account name, ARR, health score, week-over-week usage delta
productMapping.score: Card border color = health (green healthy, amber medium, red at-risk)
productMapping.act: Tap Deploy → indigo flash, '+$49K ARR Saved', 'Draft ready' floater

premise.hook: Renewal week. At-risk accounts keep surfacing faster than your team can triage.
premise.protagonist: Player = post-sales lead drowning in signals — one tap away from Quivly backup.
premise.setting: Quivly command board — dark SaaS UI, account cards pop from a signal queue (not a 3D arena).
premise.conflict: Red accounts pop up constantly. Ignore healthy ones. Miss at-risk = churn.
premise.stakes: Save ARR before the 90-second renewal timer hits zero.
premise.resolution: Every deploy shows 'Draft ready' — Quivly already wrote the save.
premise.whyItMatters: Mirrors real Quivly: surface risk, act with the draft already written.
storyBeats: Open: 'Renewal day in 90 seconds. Signals incoming.'
Play: Account cards pop — ARR, health score, usage delta visible
Deploy: Indigo pulse + '+$49K ARR Saved' + 'Draft ready'
Close: Rank title + 'See how Quivly does this for real →'

previousDirection.note: Unreal FPS arena — kept for learning, not primary ship target
previousDirection.hook: Renewal day is in 90 seconds. Accounts are slipping in Customer Command arena.

pillars: One mechanic: tap to deploy on at-risk accounts
Product metaphor in 5 seconds (PagerTron / Wizlympics bar)
Shareable score card — ARR saved + rank title
Browser-first; Unreal stays as Game Lab learning sandbox

steps: Account cards pop from signal queue: 🟢 Healthy (ignore), 🔴 At-risk (tap Deploy), 🟡 Expansion (tap for bonus ARR)
Deploy = indigo flash + chime + '+ARR Saved' + 'Draft ready' floater
Tap healthy or miss at-risk = churn tick (health bar drops)
End screen: total ARR saved, accounts rescued, streak, rank title

acts.title: The only act
acts.description: One board, one loop, replay for high score. Marketing CTA after play.

scenes.title: Signal Triage Board
beats: Title: QUIVLY — Renewal Rush · Press to start
Signal queue animates — cards pop at random holes/slots
HUD: timer + ARR saved + streak + mini health bar
End card: score breakdown + rank + share + Book a demo CTA

characters.name: You
characters.role: Post-sales lead with Quivly agents on standby

characters.name: Quivly Agent
characters.role: Deploy pulse on tap — indigo flash that saves the account and surfaces the draft

uiCopy.start: Renewal day in 90 seconds. Deploy your agents.
uiCopy.startAlt: Every signal. One place. 90 seconds to act.
uiCopy.winAlt: ARR secured — your post-sales team, without the headcount.
uiCopy.loseAlt: Risk got there first. Quivly would've had the draft ready.

marketingUse.team: Internal leaderboard, Friday fun, onboarding icebreaker
marketingUse.customers: Booth demo, webinar opener, 'feel the speed' moment

primary: Account card component: logo, ARR, health score, usage delta
Spawn logic: weighted red > amber > green; speed ramp
End screen + share screenshot + quivly.ai CTA
phase2: Sound: soft chime on deploy (PagerTron-style optional music toggle)
unrealSandbox: Keep Lvl_ArenaShooter polish as Unreal learning exercise
Do not block marketing ship on Unreal/WebGL export

openQuestions: Leaderboard: local only first, or web/API from day one?
Swag unlock at score threshold (Wizlympics-style) or pure bragging rights?
lockedDecisions: Platform: browser-first — Game Lab canvas → quivly.ai embed (confirmed)
Unreal FPS arena: learning sandbox only, not marketing ship target (confirmed)
Priority: premium dark SaaS look — Stripe meets arcade glow, not military shooter
Interaction: tap Deploy — indigo pulse feedback, not rifle / gun fantasy
Brand synced from quivly.ai landing page — forest logo, indigo accent #6366F1

visualDirection.aesthetic: Premium dark SaaS command center — Stripe meets arcade glow
visualDirection.mood: Calm confidence, fast but never chaotic

gameplayMapping.connect: Round start — signal icons flood the board from CRM, usage, support
gameplayMapping.see: Each popped card shows Customer 360: account name, ARR, contacts, usage delta
gameplayMapping.score: Card border color = health score (green / amber / red)
gameplayMapping.act: Tap Deploy on at-risk cards — Actions Feed style '+ARR Saved' + 'Draft ready'

world.board: Dark command-center panel with 3×3 or 2×4 card slots, subtle indigo grid
world.background: #0A0A0A with soft radial glow behind active slot
world.cards: Glass account tiles — company initial, ARR pill, health badge, usage sparkline
world.spawn: Cards rise from slot with spring animation — not enemy sprites

feel.input: Single click/tap — no movement, no aim
feel.feedback: Indigo deploy pulse, soft chime, floating +ARR text, brief card shrink on save
feel.motion: Subtle slot pulse on spawn; speed ramp every 15s
feel.ui: Minimal HUD — thin font, glass panels, big renewal timer, streak counter

avoid: Gun models, muzzle flash, blood, industrial grey blocks
Loud arcade chaos, cheap particle spam

unrealPolishChecklist: Enable Lumen + Virtual Shadow Maps in project settings
Add Post Process Volume: bloom, exposure, slight vignette
Replace rifle with invisible Agent Pulse (line trace + Niagara beam)
Account orbs: emissive materials + point lights
UMG HUD: Quivly colors, Inter-style typography feel
Target 60fps — small arena, few actors = smooth on Mac
